title: Simple 2x2-Framed Outdoor Coffee Table
summary: A planked 1x3 tabletop with breadboard ends, sitting on a plain 2x2 leg frame set in an inch from the edges.
description:
This is one of the more approachable outdoor tables in the catalog — nine 1x3 boards joined edge to edge with breadboard ends capping the grain, resting on a simple 2x2 box-and-leg frame. Both pocket holes and plain countersunk screws work throughout, so it's a reasonable build for someone without a pocket-hole jig yet.

The base sits centered under the top with a 1" reveal on all sides, which is enough to read as an intentional design choice rather than the base just being slightly too small. Get that gap even before you commit to final screws — it's the one thing anyone looking at the finished table will notice first.

materials.name: Pine, 1x3, 8 ft
materials.note: Use an exterior-rated species or treat/seal for outdoor use.

materials.name: 2-1/2" wood screws or pocket hole screws

steps.title: Choose your joinery method and mark holes
steps.body: This build works with either a pocket-hole jig (set for 1-1/2" stock) or plain countersunk screws. Mark and predrill all your joints now, whichever method you're using, before starting assembly.

steps.title: Build the two leg sections
steps.body: Build two leg sections from the 2x2 legs and supports with 2-1/2" screws and glue.

steps.title: Join the leg sections into the base
steps.body: Attach the two leg sections to the side aprons to complete the base, adjusting for square before the fasteners are fully driven.

steps.title: Attach the breadboard ends
steps.body: If you're not using a pocket-hole jig, attach the breadboard ends first with 2" finish nails and glue — overhang the ends by an inch, with the inside face flush to the leg section.

steps.title: Attach the tabletop boards
steps.body: If using pocket holes, drill them in all the tabletop boards (set for 3/4" stock) and attach to the breadboard ends with 1-1/4" pocket hole screws and glue, keeping the top surface flush. If you already attached the breadboard ends by hand, fasten the remaining tabletop boards to them the same way.

steps.title: Attach the base to the top
steps.body: Center the base on the underside of the finished tabletop with a 1" gap around all sides. Screw the base to the top with 2" screws and glue.
materials: 2-1/2" wood screws or pocket hole screws

images.alt: A planked outdoor coffee table with breadboard ends on a simple 2x2 leg frame
